general_goal_of_analysis: Situate Bobbio's two conceptions of democracy (ethical-participatory and procedural) within the broader traditions of democratic theory
brief_justification: This is a political theory article that analyzes Norberto Bobbio's intellectual trajectory through close reading of his texts and secondary literature. It traces his shift from ethical-participatory democracy (1940s actionist period) to procedural democracy (post-1955), situating both within broader traditions of democratic thought. No empirical data, no quantitative methods, and no causal claims are present. The method is interpretive analysis of published works and intellectual history.
